input.command:
cd /c/Users/JOSE/Downloads/tentativa-50/companion && git add web/server/cli-launcher.ts web/server/cli-launcher.test.ts && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
fix(server): use 'where' on Windows for binary path resolution

On Windows, the 'which' command doesn't exist. Changed cli-launcher to use 
platform-appropriate command: 'where' on Windows, 'which' on Unix/macOS.

Also improved path detection to use path.isAbsolute() which handles both 
Unix (/usr/bin/claude) and Windows (C:\path\to\claude.exe) absolute paths.

Added tests for both Unix and Windows behavior including Windows drive-letter 
paths.

Fixes the error "'which' não é reconhecido como um comando interno" on 
Windows systems.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"
input.description: Commit the fix with commitzen format

input.subject: Claude CLI executable exists at resolved path
input.description: The Claude Code CLI executable must be found and accessible at the path returned by the system resolver. If the resolver returns a path, that file must actually exist.

input.subject: Session spawns without ENOENT errors
input.description: Creating a new session must successfully spawn the Claude CLI process without ENOENT (no such file or directory) errors. The spawn should complete and the CLI should connect back via WebSocket.

input.subject: Claude CLI path resolution works on Windows
input.description: The path resolution mechanism (using 'where' on Windows) must correctly find the actual Claude CLI executable location on Windows systems. The resolved path must be valid and the executable must launch successfully.

input.command: where claude 2>&1 || echo "Claude not found in PATH"
input.description: Find Claude CLI location on Windows

input.command:
cd /c/Users/JOSE/Downloads/tentativa-50/companion && git add -A && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
fix(server): handle multi-line output from Windows 'where' command

On Windows, the 'where' command returns all matching paths (one per line),
not just the first match. The previous code didn't split the output, causing
the spawn command to receive a multi-line string with all paths concatenated.

Fixed by splitting the output by newlines and taking the first path, which is
the most preferred match (similar to how 'which' works on Unix).

Example 'where' output on Windows:
  C:\Users\...\npm\claude
  C:\Users\...\npm\claude.cmd
  C:\Users\...\.local\bin\claude.exe

After fix: Only uses the first line.

Added test to verify multi-line output is handled correctly.

Fixes ENOENT errors when spawning Claude CLI on Windows.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"
cd /c/Users/JOSE/Downloads/tentativa-50/companion/web && timeout 8 bun run dev 2>&1 || true
